item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Monday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
first impressions are important
good presentation can go a long way
i guess
unhinged
i see nothing going wrong with that
always happens
that's unsettling
don't get too crazy now..
looks like a very well behaved crab
so good
we've all done this
except weekends of course
is there another reason people get CT scans?
fair but why is dude pointing a gun at MJ?
as is tradition
fast
that's my set.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: I'm scared
item.description: This is in the UK. Got busted with a lot of painkillers and other drugs in Nov 22. Got processed and released on bail. Had my bail extended 3 times then released on investigation. Since I was originally released on bail I have worked hard to better my self. I moved across the country to have a better environment. I got out of the toxic manual labour industry and got a job in finance. Rent my own place, I tried to get therapy but the NHS was so slow and private is so expensive. I had a interview with the police a month ago, I admitted to the stuff being mine and explained a little why. Personal use not distribution. 2 weeks ago I had a court date, I plead guilty obviously and the prosecution wants 2 years. The magistrate court didn't think they had the ability to provide a proper sentence so they past it to the crown. So here I am, just waiting. I keep going over my mitigating circumstances. First offence, Remorse, Good character references from 6 people, I was under 25, I have autism, ADHD and PTSD, I have taken steps to address my behaviour, I have breathing issues, I had a hard upbringing, I have great prospects at my job. I can take any other punishment, Curfew, fine, reporting to a probation officer, restricted movement even suspended sentence but not actual prison. No one knows the outcome and that scares me the most, the waiting. EDIT: I appreciate everyone telling me prison is not that bad and I'll get through it but that's not really helping. I have a fear of having my movements physically restricted after being sexually abused as a kid. I also barley got this finance job and I am scared that having this on my record and the gap in my CV will make it impossible to get back in. I worked so hard to rebuild my life and I can't have it taken away again.
item.dc:creator: thetinymonarch

item.title: Goblin Queen is the gift.
item.description: Going to need Marvel give me more Goblin Queen screen time as my Xmas gift this year. I've been good, and so I would like my chaotic gift, thank you. All photos by T.D.photography on IG
item.dc:creator: Krissiq

item.title: The Definition Of Being An Adult
item.description: I made this when my mother died and recently came across it. I realize that making memes is kind of our modern way of writing poetry to capture a moment in time. If you've got a mother, call her and tell her you love her. Maybe plan a trip to Hawaii with her or something!
item.dc:creator: jlist

14
15. Hope this dump was entertaining. Also I wan to share with you that I'm working on a wacky game called Do Not Press The Button (cuz that shit might delete the multiverse, yo). If you had fun here today, please consider a wishlist on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Dont_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse_The_Game/

item.dc:creator: TapiocaMartini

item.title: Dead Punctuations
item.description: Which one would you bring back? I am partial to the "interrobang" and not because of the name but because it has a bit of an existential panic to it. Like, things are happening to you, and you've got no choice but to excitedly confirm the happenings. Here is one devastating example: "There is not going to be an Imgur Secret Santa this year (this is where I'd put an interrobang, IF I HAD ONE)"
item.dc:creator: CatfishKisser

item.title: That’s actually a good one
item.description: British mobile operator O2 has introduced a new service called "AI Grandma," which effectively wastes the time of phone scammers. Suspicious calls are automatically redirected to a voice bot that mimics a grandmother, asking endless questions and keeping scammers on the line for up to 40 minutes.
item.dc:creator: SomebodythatIusedtonope

item.title: Did you know the Cash Cab guy was a comedian too?
item.description: His name is Ben Bailey. He has a few specials out. If you're in Florida, he will be performing there in December.
item.dc:creator: MayMyEnemiesLiveLong

item.title: Sophie Lancaster (26 November 1986 - 24 August 2007)
item.description:
Walking home with her boyfriend, beaten to into a coma from which she would never recover for no reason other than she was a goth. I've been in the scene a long time, and I've had to deal with my share of harassment; stuff thrown at me from passing cars, shoved, yelled at, threatened, once had a shotgun pulled on me. There was a guy at the club I worked at in the 90s who wore a shirt that said "I was goth when it was called HEY FA@@OT" and I still think about that all the time because it still happens.
Tourists were a frequent problem. Usually your run of the mill every day looking guys come in wearing black running shoes, black jeans or track pants and a black t-shirt, too often zooted out of their gourd and looking to pick up a freaky goth chick. Usually everyone knew well enough to stay away; the cocksure smug entitlement didn't do them any favours. Those guys usually sleezed through the place hitting on any girl in reach and getting kicked out (roughly) by security when they try to shove themselves between a girl and her significant other. Some came in smooth as butter though. A friend left after spending most of the night talking and dancing with a guy, and I never saw her again. I found out after they had gone to a boozecan with a group of people she knew and they'd been sitting talking and making out the whole time so no one thought much about it when they left together. She woke up in the stairwell of a nearby building hours later, naked, covered in cigarette burns and her own blood and missing a tooth. I know most people aren't like this; there were a lot of people who didn't fit the the image of the surrounding crowd who came to my bar and were readily accepted because they weren't jerks. I would just like to take this moment remember that every Goth Girlfriend and even us guys have a heart and soul and feelings and are actual people. Sophie didn't deserve to die because she dressed different.
item.dc:creator: weave

item.title: It's too cold to handfeed so little Branwen has to pick the kibble out herself

item.title: Justice ... of a sort.
item.description:
But her children still have no mother, and her 11-year-old son, who was standing next to her when she was killed, gets to have lifelong trauma.
https://www.newser.com/story/354656/jury-convicts-white-woman-of-killing-black-neighbor.html More background: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killing_of_Ajike_Owens
item.dc:creator: TheCunningLinguists
